root_cause.text: The end pointer `src_pb` was incorrectly calculated using the variable `t` instead of the height variable `h`, resulting in an improper boundary for the image rectangle copy loop and allowing out-of-bounds array accesses.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces `t * linesize` with `h * linesize` when initializing the `src_pb` pointer, which serves as the termination condition in the `for (; src_py < src_pb; ...)` loop.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The end pointer `src_pb` was incorrectly calculated using the variable `t` instead of the height variable `h`, resulting in an improper boundary for the image rectangle copy loop and allowing out-of-bounds array accesses.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces `t * linesize` with `h * linesize` when initializing the `src_pb` pointer, which serves as the termination condition in the `for (; src_py < src_pb; ...)` loop.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix image rectangle copy bounds by using height 'h' instead of 't'.
intent_record.what: Changed the loop boundary multiplier from 't' to 'h' when calculating src_pb.
intent_record.how: Ensures the correct height is used to calculate the end pointer for the memory copy loop, preventing out-of-bounds or incomplete copies.

grouping_result.intent: Fix image rectangle copy bounds by using height 'h' instead of 't'.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly fixes the boundary calculation error described in the root cause by using the correct height variable 'h' instead of 't'.

candidates.rationale: This line calculates the end pointer src_pb using 't' instead of 'h', which directly causes the out-of-bounds boundary.

candidates.content: for (; src_py < src_pb; src_py += linesize, dst_py += linesize) {
candidates.rationale: This line uses the improperly calculated src_pb for the loop condition, leading to out-of-bounds array access.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement calculates the end pointer `src_pb` using the incorrect variable `t` instead of the height `h`, leading to an improper boundary for the image rectangle copy loop.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
gifdec: fix invalid write in gif_copy_img_rect

Signed-off-by: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 9321e93502810e4a3fcaf87bac156dba2fe3b477
Author: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Dec 20 17:56:52 2012 +0000

    gifdec: fix invalid write in gif_copy_img_rect
    
    Signed-off-by: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>

diff --git a/libavcodec/gifdec.c b/libavcodec/gifdec.c
index 59574e934b..9a3aef075f 100644
--- a/libavcodec/gifdec.c
+++ b/libavcodec/gifdec.c
@@ -118,7 +118,7 @@ static void gif_copy_img_rect(const uint32_t *src, uint32_t *dst,
     const uint32_t *src_px, *src_p
bic_trace_step.reason: The diff clearly shows the modification to the calculation of `src_pb`, changing `src_py + (t + h) * linesize` to `src_py + t * linesize`, which corrects the boundary calculation and fixes the out-of-bounds access.
